_comment: Guardrail + budget policy for the Command Center orchestrator. JSON, not YAML: stdlib-only parsing (json module), matching the fleet_bus.py 'pure stdlib, runs on every fleet machine' convention. Human-reviewable; the loop reads this every cycle, never hardcodes these numbers in code.
_note_spawn_worker: spawn_worker is green ONLY while under concurrency_cap AND under the budget warn threshold below — guardrail.py enforces this, it is not a blanket green. Exceeding either reclassifies the same action as spawn_beyond_cap (red).

budget._note_max_cycles: Safety ceiling, ~2x the normal rate. At cycle_interval_seconds=1800 the loop runs 48 cycles/day, i.e. steady-state 50% budget. This was 48 originally — exactly equal to the daily cycle count, so the budget guardrail would have false-alarmed at 100% every single day. Caught during a v2 review, before an always-on install.

denied_permissions_note: Enforced at the settings.json permission layer for the orchestrator's own Claude session, see ../settings.orchestrator.json — this policy file governs the deterministic loop's own action classification, the settings.json is the hard technical backstop for anything that DOES invoke Claude.
